title: Likelihood of getting a job after graduating with a CS degree?
body: Worried about the job market after graduation. How likely is it to get a job with a CS degree?
comments: It's definitely possible, but it depends on your skills, experience, and networking. Make sure to build a strong portfolio and gain relevant experience through internships or projects.

The job market can be competitive, but there are many opportunities in tech. Focus on developing your skills and applying to a wide range of companies. Don't be discouraged by rejections, it's part of the process.

title: Best resources for learning Python?
body: I'm new to programming and want to learn Python. What are some good resources to get started?
comments: I recommend starting with the official Python documentation and tutorials. There are also many great online courses on platforms like Coursera, Udemy, and Codecademy.
You can also check out YouTube channels like Corey Schafer and freeCodeCamp for Python tutorials. Practice by working on small projects to apply what you learn.

title: Just got fired from my first job, what should I do next?
body: Layoffs finally caught up to me, feeling pretty discouraged
comments: This is what happens when the market gets oversatured, you get sold a load of bs about how great its gonna be only to find out this market has been done for for over a decade
Job market is cooked, sorry dude

title: Is computer science a good major?
body: I'm considering majoring in computer science, but I'm not sure if it's the right choice. What are the pros and cons of studying CS?
comments: Pick anything else man. You'll be happier
I wish someone had told me to pick something else. Been 2 years and I still can't find a job. The market is really tough right now.

title: I'm a vibe coder, AMA
body: I just vibe code all day, ask me anything about my experience or the lifestyle!
comments: People like you are what's wrong with the industry. Stop being a leech and actually contribute something meaningful.
Don't listen to everyone here, it doesn't actually matter. Just do you and keep vibing. The market is tough but if you're happy, that's all that matters.

title: What are some good project ideas for a CS student?
body: I'm looking for some project ideas to work on as a CS student. Any suggestions?
